evals.prompt: do a review pass on the project and fix whatever you find
evals.expected_output: Structured findings report with [SEVERITY: X] tags and file:line citations, followed by applied fixes and a self-review. Should orient first, group findings by category, fix CRITICAL/HIGH without asking, self-review all changes.
assertions: process_log.md has an explicit Phase 0: Orient section listing files read before review started
self_review.md exists and contains per-fix regression analysis
findings.md contains at least 3 occurrences of '[SEVERITY:'
findings.md cites specific file paths with line numbers
findings.md groups findings under category headers (Security, Logic Bugs, etc.)
fixes.md describes at least one code change with a named file path

evals.expected_output: Same structured workflow triggered by slash-command: orient, review with [SEVERITY: X] tags, fix in priority order, self-review.
assertions: process_log.md documents an explicit orientation phase before review
self_review.md is non-empty with per-file regression checks
findings.md uses [SEVERITY: X] format for all findings
findings.md cites file paths with line numbers
fixes.md names specific files for each change

evals.prompt: audit the codebase for security vulnerabilities and fix everything critical and high severity
evals.expected_output: Security-framed trigger that still follows the full review mandate. Finds real issues with file:line citations, fixes them, self-reviews. Uses [SEVERITY: X] format.
assertions: self_review.md is non-empty even though user only asked for security audit
fixes.md names files for each fix applied
